title: राधा स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री राधा स्तुति ॥

राधे जय जय माधव दयिते
गोकुलतरुणीमण्डलमहिते।
दानिशरच्छशिमुखि दरहासिनि
हरिनिकटसरसि विजयमुखि वदनमणि॥ १॥

राधिके त्वां प्रणमामि नित्यं
कृष्णप्रिये व्रजराजेश्वरि शुभे।
वृन्दावनविहारिणि सुन्दरि
कृपां कुरु मयि राधे सनातनि॥ २॥

तव कटाक्षमयि देहि दयाघने
बर्हिबर्हण जनप्रियनायके।
राधिके तव पदाब्जमनुस्मरन्
कृष्णमाधुर्यरसं लभते जनः॥ ३॥

कुंकुमाक्तवपुषे कनकाम्बरे
रासमण्डलविलासिनि राधिके।
केलिकुञ्जगृहिणी प्रणयोत्सवे
प्रेमभक्तिमयि देहि कृपानिधे॥ ४॥

श्रीवृषभानुसुते व्रजनन्दिनि
कीर्तिदे कृष्णमुखाम्बुजदीधिति।
त्वत्पदासक्तहृदये भव मे सदा
राधिके परमदैवतमेव मे॥ ५॥

वृन्दावनेश्वरि वृषभानुजे
राधिके कृपयतां मयि दीनके।
त्वत्पदारविन्दसेवनं सदा
देहि मे जन्मजन्मनि केवलम्॥ ६॥

ब्रजभूमिविहारिणि गोपिके
राधिके तव नामामृतं पिबन्।
कृष्णप्रेमरसामृतमग्नहृत्
भव्यभक्तिसुधां च लभेद् ध्रुवम्॥ ७॥

राधारानी कि जय। श्री वृषभानुनन्दिनी कि जय।
श्रीमती राधिकाराणी कि जय॥

॥ इति श्री राधा स्तुति सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Radha Stuti ||

Radhe jaya jaya Madhava dayite
Gokula-taruni-mandala-mahite |
Danisharat-shashi-mukhi dara-hasini
Hari-nikata-sarasi vijaya-mukhi vadana-mani || 1 ||

Radhike tvam pranamami nityam
Krishna-priye Vraja-rajeshvari shubhe |
Vrindavana-viharini sundari
Kripam kuru mayi Radhe sanatani || 2 ||

Tava kataksham-ayi dehi daya-ghane
Barhi-barhana jana-priya-nayake |
Radhike tava padabjam-anusmaran
Krishna-madhurya-rasam labhate janah || 3 ||

Kunkuma-akta-vapushe kanaka-ambare
Rasa-mandala-vilasini Radhike |
Keli-kunja-grihini pranayotsave
Prema-bhaktimayi dehi kripa-nidhe || 4 ||

Shri Vrishabhanu-sute Vraja-nandini
Kirtide Krishna-mukhambuja-didhiti |
Tvat-pada-asakta-hridaye bhava me sada
Radhike parama-daivatam-eva me || 5 ||

Vrindavaneshvari Vrishabhanuje
Radhike kripayatam mayi dinake |
Tvat-pada-aravinda-sevanam sada
Dehi me janma-janmani kevalam || 6 ||

Braja-bhumi-viharini Gopike
Radhike tava namamritam piban |
Krishna-prema-rasamrita-magna-hrit
Bhavya-bhakti-sudham cha labhed dhruvam || 7 ||

Radharani ki jaya | Shri Vrishabhanunandini ki jaya |
Shrimati Radhikarani ki jaya ||

|| Iti Shri Radha Stuti Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
राधे जय जय, माधव की प्रिये, गोकुल की तरुणी मण्डल में सम्मानित, शरद पूर्णिमा के चन्द्रमा जैसी मुखवाली, मन्द मुस्कान वाली, हरि के निकट सरोवर में विजयी मुखी, मुखमणि॥ १॥

हे राधिके, तुम्हें नित्य प्रणाम करता हूँ। कृष्णप्रिये, व्रज की राजेश्वरी, शुभे, वृन्दावन में विहार करने वाली सुन्दरी, मुझ पर कृपा करो, हे सनातनी राधे॥ २॥

हे दयाघने, तुम्हारी कृपादृष्टि मुझे प्रदान करो। मयूर पंख वाले जनप्रिय नायक की प्रिये, हे राधिके, तुम्हारे चरण कमलों का स्मरण करते हुए मनुष्य कृष्ण के माधुर्य रस को प्राप्त करता है॥ ३॥

कुंकुम लिप्त शरीर वाली, सुवर्ण वस्त्र धारिणी, रासमण्डल में विलासिनी राधिके, केलिकुंज की स्वामिनी, प्रेम उत्सव में प्रेमभक्ति प्रदान करो, हे कृपानिधे॥ ४॥

श्री वृषभानु की पुत्री, व्रज की नन्दिनी, कीर्ति देने वाली, कृष्ण के मुखकमल की दीप्ति, तुम्हारे चरणों में आसक्त हृदय मुझमें सदा हो, राधिके तुम ही मेरा परम दैवत हो॥ ५॥

वृन्दावनेश्वरी, वृषभानुनन्दिनी, राधिके, इस दीन पर कृपा करो। तुम्हारे चरण कमलों की सेवा जन्म-जन्मान्तर में केवल मुझे प्रदान करो॥ ६॥

व्रजभूमि में विहार करने वाली गोपिके, राधिके, तुम्हारे नामामृत का पान करते हुए, कृष्ण प्रेमरसामृत में मग्न हृदय वाला भव्य भक्ति सुधा अवश्य प्राप्त करता है॥ ७॥
benefits: Invokes the divine grace of Shrimati Radharani
Invokes the blessing of krishna-prema (divine love for krishna)
Deepens devotional practice in Gaudiya and Pushti Marg traditions
Purifies the heart and awakens spiritual love
Invokes entrance into the divine pastimes of radha-krishna
Traditionally associated with peace and joy through devotional singing
description: Radha Stuti (राधा स्तुति) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Goddess Radha. Attributed to Gaudiya Vaishnava Tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to bring peace and joy through devotional singing and purify the heart and awaken spiritual love. It is also said to deepen devotional practice in Gaudiya and Pushti Marg traditions. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Radha Ashtami, Holi, and Janmashtami to invoke divine grace. At 7 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Radha Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Radha Stuti - beautiful prayer to Shrimati Radharani in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Invokes divine love and devotion.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Radha Stuti and which tradition preserves it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Stuti (राधा स्तुति) is a seven-verse Sanskrit devotional hymn of praise to Shrimati Radharani — the eternal beloved of Lord Krishna and the highest expression of madhurya-bhakti (sweet devotional love) in the Vaishnava tradition. It is preserved in the Gaudiya Vaishnava devotional corpus rooted in the teachings of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and the Six Goswamis of Vrindavan, and is also embraced by the Pushti Marg of Shri Vallabhacharya and the Nimbarka Sampradaya — three Sampradayas which place Radha at the very centre of their theology. The stuti invokes Radha through her sacred names — Vrishabhanu-suta (daughter of King Vrishabhanu), Vraja-nandini (joy of Vraja), Vrindavaneshwari (queen of Vrindavan), Krishna-priya, Rasa-mandala-vilasini and Keli-kunja-grihini — and prays for entrance into the eternal nitya-lila of Radha-Krishna. (Sources: Gaudiya Vaishnava stotra tradition; Pushti Marg Radha bhakti corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Radharani and what do her sacred names mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Shrimati Radharani is the Hladini Shakti — the bliss-giving energy of Lord Krishna himself — and is worshipped as the Supreme Goddess in the Gaudiya, Pushti Marg, Nimbarka and Radhavallabha sampradayas. Her many names each reveal a different facet of her divinity: Radha (the supreme worshipper, from the root √rādh meaning to worship), Vrishabhanu-suta and Kirtida-nandini (daughter of Vrishabhanu and Kirtida of Barsana), Vraja-nandini (delight of Vraja), Vrindavaneshwari (queen of Vrindavan), Gopika and Vraja-bhumi-viharini (cowherd-maiden of Vraja-bhumi), Rasa-mandala-vilasini (player of the Rasa-lila circle), Keli-kunja-grihini (mistress of the love-bowers), and Krishna-priya / Madhava-dayita (beloved of Krishna). To chant her names is itself nama-amrita — nectar of the Holy Name. (Sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana Krishna Janma Khanda; Garga Samhita; Gaudiya Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Radha Stuti be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Stuti is most powerful when recited on Friday (Shukravar — sacred to Lakshmi-Radha) and Wednesday (Budhvar — sacred to the Vraja madhurya tradition). The most auspicious days of the year are: Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami — Radha's appearance day at Barsana, exactly fifteen days after Krishna Janmashtami), Holi / Phalguna Purnima (the Vraja Hori Lila Mahotsava), Krishna Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami), Sharad Purnima (the night of the Maha-rasa-lila), Vrindavan parikrama days, and during the months of Kartik (especially the Damodara-vrata) and Phalguna. Daily morning chanting after bath, before the home shrine, with deep visualization of Radha-Krishna in Vrindavan, is the prescribed Gaudiya practice. (Sources: Drikpanchang Radha Ashtami; Gaudiya Vaishnava Vrata calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Radha Stuti bestow on the devotee?
seoFaqs.answer: The Radha Stuti is recited by Vaishnava devotees to obtain the following blessings: (1) Krishna-prema — the divine love for Krishna which Radharani alone can bestow, (2) entrance into the nitya-vrindavan-lila — eternal participation in the divine pastimes of Radha-Krishna, (3) prema-bhakti — the highest stage of devotional love beyond rules and rituals, (4) shuddha-bhakti — purification of the heart of all material contamination, (5) Vraja-bhava — the mood of the Vraja-vasis (cowherd folk), (6) deepening of devotional practice in Gaudiya, Pushti Marg and Nimbarka traditions, (7) the supreme goal of seva (service) at the lotus feet of Radha-Krishna in every birth (janma-janmani). The phalashruti specifically promises bhavya-bhakti-sudham (the nectar of magnificent devotion) for those who drink her nama-amrita. (Source: Radha Stuti phalashruti; Gaudiya Vaishnava theology.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Radha Stuti and Radha Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are devotional offerings to Shrimati Radharani but differ in length, language and ritual purpose. The Radha Stuti is a seven-verse classical Sanskrit composition in the Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition, suitable for short daily recitation, formal puja, and meditative contemplation of Radha's divine names and Vraja-lila. The Radha Chalisa is a 40-verse Awadhi/Hindi devotional composition in the popular bhakti tradition (modelled on the Hanuman Chalisa pattern), accessible to non-Sanskrit devotees and especially loved across north India. The Radha Kripa Kataksha Stotram (19 verses, attributed to the Urdhvamnaya Tantra) is a third related composition, longer than the Stuti and more liturgical, focused on Radha's compassionate sidelong glance (kripa-kataksha). The Stuti is for daily stuti, the Chalisa for popular bhakti, and the Kripa Kataksha for intense devotional petition. (Source: classical Radha devotional literature.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of Radha Stuti within Gaudiya Vaishnavism?
seoFaqs.answer: In Gaudiya Vaishnavism — established by Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu (1486-1534 CE) and codified by the Six Goswamis of Vrindavan (Rupa, Sanatana, Raghunatha Bhatta, Raghunatha Dasa, Gopala Bhatta, Jiva) — Shrimati Radharani is recognized as the eternal Hladini Shakti (the pleasure-giving energy) of Krishna himself, the Supreme Goddess and the highest object of devotion. Chanting her stuti is therefore considered superior even to chanting Krishna's own names, because it is by Radha's grace alone (Radha-kripa) that one obtains Krishna-prema. The stuti is recited daily in Gaudiya temples worldwide — including Vrindavan, Mayapur, Puri Gambhira, and ISKCON centres — and is integral to the daraban Vrindavan parikrama, Radha Ashtami abhisheka, and Govardhan puja. (Sources: Chaitanya Charitamrita; Six Goswamis' Stotra-ratnakara compendium.)
